label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure out this problem step by step. So there are five people: Alice, Bob, Claire, Dave, and Eve. Each starts with a present of a different color. The initial gifts are: - Alice: brown present - Bob: orange ball - Claire: purple present - Dave: yellow present - Eve: white present Then there are a series of swaps. Let me track each swap and see who ends up with which present. First swap: Bob and Eve swap their gifts. So before this swap: - Bob has orange ball - Eve has white present After swapping: - Bob has white present - Eve has orange ball Second swap: Bob and Claire swap their gifts. Before this swap: - Bob has white present - Claire has purple present After swapping: - Bob has purple present - Claire has white present Third swap: Dave and Alice swap their gifts. Before this swap: - Dave has yellow present - Alice has brown present After swapping: - Dave has brown present - Alice has yellow present Fourth swap: Bob and Eve swap their gifts again. Before this swap: - Bob has purple present - Eve has orange ball (from the first swap) After swapping: - Bob has orange ball - Eve has purple present Fifth swap: Alice and Bob swap their gifts. Before this swap: - Alice has yellow present - Bob has orange ball After swapping: - Alice has orange ball - Bob has yellow present So at the end, Bob has the yellow present. Let me double-check to make sure I didn't make any mistakes. Initial: - Alice: brown - Bob: orange - Claire: purple - Dave: yellow - Eve: white After first swap (Bob-Eve): - Alice: brown - Bob: white - Claire: purple - Dave: yellow - Eve: orange After second swap (Bob-Claire): - Alice: brown - Bob: purple - Claire: white - Dave: yellow - Eve: orange After third swap (Dave-Alice): - Alice: yellow - Bob: purple - Claire: white - Dave: brown - Eve:
